summary: Data class var property used as map key causes hashCode corruption; data class inheriting from open class creates asymmetric equals contract.

findings.evidence: `CacheKey.id` is declared as `var`; `registerKey` mutates `key.id` after inserting it into `keyCache`, invalidating the original hashCode and causing silent map lookup failures.
findings.minimal_fix: Change `var id: String` to `val id: String` in `CacheKey`. If the id must be updated post-construction, use `copy(id = "resolved-...")` and re-insert the new key into the map.
findings.verification: Add a test: insert a `CacheKey` into a map, call `registerKey`, then look up the original key and assert it is found. Confirm the test passes after the fix.

findings.evidence: `ClickEvent` (data class) extends `BaseEvent` (open class); `ClickEvent.equals/hashCode` only considers `elementId` and `timestamp`, ignoring `id` and `type` from `BaseEvent`. Two `ClickEvent` instances with different ids but same `elementId` and `timestamp` are incorrectly considered equal.
findings.minimal_fix: Replace `open class BaseEvent` with `sealed class BaseEvent` and include `id` and `type` as properties of each data subclass, ensuring all fields participate in `equals`/`hashCode`.
findings.verification: Create two `ClickEvent` instances with different `id` values but same `elementId` and `timestamp`; assert they are NOT equal. Verify the assertion passes after the fix.
